name: T10 VC4
description: Quad linear DC-coupled VCA. Four identical channels with individual outputs, A+C and B+D mix outputs, and +5V-normalled CV inputs for manual-open behavior when unpatched.

inputs.name: CV A
inputs.description: CV input for channel A. Normalled high when unpatched.

inputs.name: CV B
inputs.description: CV input for channel B. Normalled high when unpatched.

inputs.name: CV C
inputs.description: CV input for channel C. Normalled high when unpatched.

inputs.name: CV D
inputs.description: CV input for channel D. Normalled high when unpatched.

outputs.name: OUT A
outputs.description: Individual output for channel A. Still contributes to the mix outputs when patched.

outputs.name: OUT B
outputs.description: Individual output for channel B. Still contributes to the mix outputs when patched.

outputs.name: OUT C
outputs.description: Individual output for channel C. Still contributes to the mix outputs when patched.

outputs.name: OUT D
outputs.description: Individual output for channel D. Still contributes to the mix outputs when patched.

outputs.name: A+C MIX
outputs.description: Mix of channels A and C.

outputs.name: B+D MIX
outputs.description: Mix of channels B and D, with A+C inverted into the bus when A+C is left unpatched.

behaviors.name: Four linear VCAs
behaviors.description: The module behaves as four independent linear VCAs. Each channel is DC coupled, so it is equally happy with audio, envelopes or slow control voltages.

behaviors.name: Open when unpatched
behaviors.description: Each CV input is normalled to +5V. That means an unpatched channel is fully open and acts like a straight-through mixer path until CV is inserted.

behaviors.name: A+C subgroup
behaviors.description: Channels A and C sum to a dedicated subgroup output. That makes the left pair an instant stereo or stem bus when you want two voices glued together.

behaviors.name: B+D subgroup
behaviors.description: Channels B and D form the second subgroup output. When A+C is left unpatched, those channels are inverted into the B+D bus for a wider composite mix.

behaviors.name: Individual taps stay live
behaviors.description: Taking an individual output does not remove the channel from the mix outputs. You can split a channel to a separate destination and keep it in the summed bus at the same time.

patchIdeas.name: Four-voice VCA mix
patchIdeas.patch: Four voices into IN A-D · four envelopes into CV A-D · A+C MIX to the left bus · B+D MIX to the right bus · a tiny quad stem mixer with no menu layer.
patchIdeas.diagram:
T10 VC4:

VCO 1:
VCO 2:
VCO 3:
VCO 4:
Envelope 1:
Envelope 2:
Envelope 3:
Envelope 4:
Output:

- VCO 1 (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN A)
- VCO 2 (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN B)
- VCO 3 (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN C)
- VCO 4 (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN D)
- Envelope 1 (Out) >> T10 VC4 (CV A)
- Envelope 2 (Out) >> T10 VC4 (CV B)
- Envelope 3 (Out) >> T10 VC4 (CV C)
- Envelope 4 (Out) >> T10 VC4 (CV D)
- T10 VC4 (A+C MIX) -> Output (L)
- T10 VC4 (B+D MIX) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo drum stem
patchIdeas.patch: Kick and snare on A/B · hats and percussion on C/D · use the two mix buses as left/right stems · the individual outs remain available for separate processing.
patchIdeas.diagram:
T10 VC4:

Kick:
Snare:
Hats:
Perc:
Output:

- Kick (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN A)
- Snare (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN B)
- Hats (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN C)
- Perc (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN D)
- T10 VC4 (OUT A) -> Output (Kick tap)
- T10 VC4 (OUT B) -> Output (Snare tap)
- T10 VC4 (A+C MIX) -> Output (L)
- T10 VC4 (B+D MIX) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Manual-open modulation hub
patchIdeas.patch: Leave CV A-D empty to open all four channels · patch audio or CV into the inputs · use the module as a passive-feeling four-channel level hub with the +5V normals doing the lifting.
patchIdeas.diagram:
T10 VC4:

LFO 1:
LFO 2:
Filter:
Output:

- LFO 1 (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN A)
- LFO 2 (Out) -> T10 VC4 (IN C)
- T10 VC4 (OUT A) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- T10 VC4 (OUT C) >> Output (Pan CV)
- T10 VC4 (A+C MIX) -> Output (In)
